location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in business operations, financial planning & analysis, or a related analytical role
Base pay range $88,560 - $110,700 consistent with mid-senior IC in Nashville
Owns financial modeling and fleet management strategy independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Finance, Business, Economics, Accounting, or a related field
5+ years of experience in business operations, financial planning & analysis, or a related analytical role—ideally in the automotive industry with exposure to autonomous vehicles
Advanced proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets including complex financial modeling, scenario analysis, and data manipulation
Strong foundation in accounting principles, financial reporting, and variance analysis
Experience with month-end close processes, accruals, and reconciliation workflows
Demonstrated ability to build and maintain financial models from scratch in a fast-paced, evolving environment
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present complex analyses to senior leadership
Comfort operating in ambiguity and building processes where none exist
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to distill complex analyses into clear, executive-ready insights
Exceptional attention to detail and a track record of producing accurate, reliable analytical work
Ability to manage multiple high priority demands simultaneously; ability to achieve success in situations with high degrees of variation and complexity

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in fleet management, rideshare, rental, or autonomous vehicles
Familiarity with vehicle remarketing, wholesale markets, or fleet lifecycle economics
Experience with BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Mode) or SQL for ad-hoc data analysis
Proficient in Salesforce generating and customizing reports and dashboards
Background working in a high-growth or startup environment where you built systems from zero
Background working on cross-functional initiatives that required aligning operational, financial, and strategic objectives

scope.surface: Flexdrive fleet management business (rental + AV), ~15,000 vehicles, US and Europe operations
scope.business_model: B2B fleet management subsidiary of Lyft; serves rideshare and autonomous vehicle fleet operators
primary_metrics_inferred: P&L forecast accuracy (forecast vs. actual variance)
core_responsibilities: Build and maintain complex financial models that forecast P&L impacts with accuracy
Analyze performance trends across business lines identifying patterns and drivers for strategic decisions
Monitor industry conditions, market dynamics, and competitive landscape
Produce and present regular performance reporting with forecast to actual variance attribution
Own and optimize core business processes across fleet operations, vendor management, and financial operations
Review actual spend against forecast on a recurring basis, identifying variances and driving root-cause analysis
Identify anomalies in complex invoice workflows and resolve discrepancies cross-functionally
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and tracking mechanisms for leadership visibility
Support month-end close deliverables including fleet/operational reporting, accruals, reconciliations, and variance analysis
Develop cost-per-mile models, depot cost analyses, and unit economics frameworks for AV operations
Serve as connective tissue between Business Operations, Finance, Fleet Operations, and external partners

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership scope described in detail
Bonus points section clearly separated from required experience — reduces ambiguity
Exposure to emerging AV fleet management space offers career growth
Strong benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave and 401(k) match
Hybrid schedule clearly defined (3 days/week Mon, Wed, Thu)
red_flags: Candidates must reside in states registered for Flexdrive Services LLC — limits applicant pool and may exclude some remote-friendly states
Hybrid office requirement is contingent on 'establishment of a Lyft Flexdrive hub or office in Nashville' — office may not yet exist, creating timeline ambiguity
No mention of visa sponsorship despite open work_auth language — left as unclear
Role spans fleet ops, financial ops, accounting, AV buildout, and cross-functional strategy — very broad scope for a single IC role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + comp range)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false from IC-level responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship from 'candidates must reside in states registered for Flexdrive Services LLC' language; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: Hybrid office in Nashville is contingent on future establishment of a Flexdrive hub — it is unclear if the office currently exists or when it will open, making the hybrid schedule timeline uncertain.

Work auth requirement ('candidates must reside in states registered for Flexdrive Services LLC') implies US residency constraint but does not explicitly state no sponsorship — treated as no-sponsorship but flagged.

JD states 'ideally in the automotive industry with exposure to autonomous vehicles' within what appears to be a required experience bullet — treated as a qualifier/preference within a hard requirement rather than a separate nice-to-have.
